key_risks: The FOMC statement or press conference could be more hawkish than expected, pushing yields higher and reversing high-duration growth and biotech exposure.
Semiconductor and Korea/Taiwan exposures could suffer sharp profit-taking after very strong recent gains.
The reported U.S.-Iran peace deal or Strait of Hormuz reopening prospects could deteriorate, reigniting geopolitical risk-off behavior.
A weaker-than-expected U.S. retail sales or jobless-claims print could undermine risk appetite before the exit close.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest liquid AI-related U.S. equity expression with exceptional 7-day and 30-day momentum; likely to benefit if risk appetite persists after the reported geopolitical de-escalation.
Semiconductor-heavy country exposure with very strong recent momentum and direct linkage to global AI hardware demand.
Highest recent trailing return in the universe and leveraged to semiconductors, exports, and global risk-on flows, offering potential short-window continuation.
Broad U.S. momentum exposure captures trend persistence while diversifying beyond a single technology subsector or country.
High-beta risk-on exposure with strong recent weekly performance and potential support if rates do not spike after the FOMC meeting.

portfolio_rationale: The portfolio leans into the strongest near-term risk-on and AI/semiconductor momentum areas, which have been outperforming the S&P 500 and could continue over a short holiday-shortened week. It diversifies across U.S. semiconductors, Taiwan, South Korea, broad momentum, and biotech while remaining intentionally high beta to maximize expected one-week alpha.
rationale_summary: Reported Middle East de-escalation, falling volatility, and strong recent equity momentum favor concentrated cyclical growth and semiconductor-linked exposure. The main bet is short-term trend continuation rather than defensive positioning.
